prompt: From the following VCF record, what is the total site-level read depth (from the INFO field) and the sample-level read depth for IRIS_155 (from the FORMAT field)? Are these the same thing?
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_155	IRIS_156
chr03	28401928	.	T	G	6420	PASS	AF=0.33;AN=6020;DP=89240	GT:DP:GQ	0/1:31:88	0/0:27:95
correct_answer: Site-level DP (INFO) = 89240, which is the total read depth summed across all samples in the cohort. Sample-level DP (FORMAT) for IRIS_155 = 31, which is the read depth for that individual sample only. They are not the same -- INFO/DP is the cohort total, FORMAT/DP is per-sample.
rubric: Must distinguish INFO/DP=89240 from FORMAT/DP=31 and explain the difference (cohort vs per-sample).
notes: Tests the critical distinction between site-level (INFO) and sample-level (FORMAT) DP fields.
